chunk_id: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc 2024.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc 2024.docx
text:
Postdoctoral fellowship in spatial biology 
Are you passionate about the tumor microenvironment and spatial biology in cancer? Join us and experience Finland, the happiest country in the world! In Helsinki, you will find an international dynamic culture combined with an excellent quality of life close to nature and with the cleanest air in the world.
Our research 
A postdoctoral position around tumor microenvironment and spatial omics analysis is now open in Anniina Färkkilä’s lab (www.farkkilab.org), located at Biomedicum, University of Helsinki, Finland. The lab is part of the Research Program for Systems Oncology, Faculty of Medicine, and FIMM, Helsinki Institute for Life Sciences. 
The overall goal is to discover how the tumor cells evade the immune system in ovarian cancer, with prospects of leading to major scientific breakthroughs and successful translation of the findings into clinical trials. We have collected a unique set of data from more than 500 patients in the prospective Oncosys-OVA study. This dataset includes multi-omics, multi-site spatial profiling data, along with whole exome- and RNA-sequencing, highly multiplexed imaging, and curated clinical data. Our multidisciplinary team has also developed experimental and functional models to validate findings emerging from the data, generating a unique environment to drive translational scientific discoveries with high clinical relevance. We have demonstrated optimizing and clinically implementing a genetic biomarker for ovarian cancer patients, that is currently used for centralized national testing in Finland (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36581696/)
Our lab received a prestigious ERC grant in 2022 and has received steady funding from e.g.

chunk_id: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc 2024.docx::chunk_0001
source_file: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc 2024.docx
text:
a genetic biomarker for ovarian cancer patients, that is currently used for centralized national testing in Finland (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36581696/)
Our lab received a prestigious ERC grant in 2022 and has received steady funding from e.g. the Research Council of Finland, Sigrid Jusélius Foundation, and the Cancer Foundation Finland. We also actively collaborate with esteemed research institutes globally, including Harvard Medical School and Netherlands Cancer Institute. 
Your mission
This position is perfect for someone aspiring to become a future group leader. We are seeking a candidate with expertise in imaging, spatial biology, transcriptomics, or proteomics. You will spearhead the development of innovative methods in spatial biology and proteomics, contributing to high-quality translational research. The project details will be customized to align with your skills and interests, aiming to find the intersection between your expertise and our research focus.
Requirements: Doctoral degree in relevant fields e.g. 
Requirements: PhD degree in 
 Histopathology
 Image analysis
 Tumor biology and immunology
 Bioinformatics
 Single cell, spatial or proteomics
What we offer:
We are an interdisciplinary team dedicated to improving cancer treatment and patient outcomes. Our flexible, collaborative approach prioritizes efficient teamwork and fosters a vibrant community, with various activities showcased on our lab's Twitter account (https://x.com/systemtumor).
Joining us means engaging in a dynamic research culture with exceptional training and mentorship to support your ambitious career goals. You’ll work alongside a multidisciplinary team, mentor students, contribute to impactful publications, and gain leadership experience.

chunk_id: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc 2024.docx::chunk_0002
source_file: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc 2024.docx
text:
ging in a dynamic research culture with exceptional training and mentorship to support your ambitious career goals. You’ll work alongside a multidisciplinary team, mentor students, contribute to impactful publications, and gain leadership experience. This postdoctoral role provides a solid foundation for future career advancement.
Our lab's collaborative atmosphere and global scientific networks drive research that directly aligns with clinical and immuno-oncological fields, focusing on translating discoveries into clinical biomarkers for patient benefit. We do not use animal models in our work.
The position is available from January 2025 onwards depending on the selected candidate’s availability. The project offers an initial fixed-term contract of three years, with a trial period of six months. A monthly salary and benefits according to the Helsinki University standards will be applied ranging from 3700 to 4000€ per month guaranteeing a good quality of life in Helsinki.
To apply, send your application at:
https://jobs.helsinki.fi/job/Helsinki-Postdoctoral-Fellowship-in-Spatial-Biology-and-Multiomics/807561402/
1. Cover letter including three referees (max 1 page)
2. CV (max 2 pages)
3. Publication list
For further information contact anniina.farkkila(at)Helsinki.fi

chunk_id: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc call chatgpt assisted.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc call chatgpt assisted.docx
text:
Postdoctoral Fellowship in Spatial Biology of Tumor Ecosystems
Are you passionate about the tumor microenvironment and spatial biology in cancer? Join us in our mission to advance ovarian cancer research through groundbreaking spatial biology innovations.
Position Details:
- Fully salaried 3-year postdoctoral position in the Research Program in Systems Oncology, Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki.
 - Application Deadline 30.11.2024
About Our Lab:
The Färkkilä Lab is at the forefront of high-impact cancer research, focusing on high-grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC). We utilize cutting-edge spatial profiling technologies to investigate the complex interactions within the tumor microenvironment (TME). By integrating advanced imaging, robust bioinformatics, and tumor organoid models, our goal is to uncover novel immunomodulatory pathways and advance precision oncology.
Our lab fosters a collaborative and vibrant research culture, combining rigorous science with team-building activities. We emphasize efficient collaborations and flexible work hours to promote a healthy work-life balance. We do not conduct animal research, supporting our animal-friendly values. Discover more about our team spirit and ongoing projects on Twitter (https://x.com/systemtumor), and our website (https://farkkilab.org/).
Your Mission:
This postdoctoral position is tailored for an ambitious scientist eager to develop novel methods in spatial biology, aiming for a future group leader position.
- Method Development Focus: You will have the freedom to design and implement innovative spatial analysis techniques, contributing to advancements in cancer research.

chunk_id: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc call chatgpt assisted.docx::chunk_0001
source_file: Hiring/Experimental postdoc 2024/Experimental postdoc call chatgpt assisted.docx
text:
velop novel methods in spatial biology, aiming for a future group leader position.
- Method Development Focus: You will have the freedom to design and implement innovative spatial analysis techniques, contributing to advancements in cancer research.
- Scientific Independence: The project will be personalized to align with your expertise, exploring intersections between your skills and our research focus.
 - Leadership Opportunities: Oversee and mentor a diverse team, gaining valuable project management and leadership experience.
- High-Impact Research: Leverage extensive resources, including clinical trial samples and a wealth of matched and paired datasets, to generate influential publications.
- Multidisciplinary Environment: Collaborate with experts in tumor biology, histopathology, image and transcriptomics analysis, as well as clinicians.
Key Responsibilities:
- Develop and lead projects centered on innovative spatial biology methods, driving new understanding of the TME.
- Collaborate in an interdisciplinary team, utilizing advanced imaging and bioinformatics tools to study tumor-immune interactions.
- Mentor students from various backgrounds and contribute to producing high-impact publications.
- Foster collaborations that advance the lab’s mission and ensure successful project outcomes.
Desired Qualifications:
- PhD in a relevant field (e.g. tumor biology, immunology, histopathology, or computational biology).
- Expertise in imaging technologies, single-cell analysis, proteomics, or spatial profiling.
- Strong proficiency in bioinformatics and data analysis, with experience handling complex biological datasets.
- Collaborative attitude, and the drive to pursue scientific independence.
What We Offer:

- Strong proficiency in bioinformatics and data analysis, with experience handling complex biological datasets.
- Collaborative attitude, and the drive to pursue scientific independence.
What We Offer: 
- Flexible Work Environment: We prioritize efficient collaborations over rigid working hours, and offer flexible working hours to support productivity and well-being.
- Career Advancement: Gain hands-on experience in leadership, mentorship, and project management in a dynamic setting.
- Scientific Freedom: Enjoy the opportunity to develop and refine new spatial biology methods with a high degree of autonomy.
 - Collaborative Team Culture: Be part of a supportive, diverse team that values both impactful science and a balanced life.
- Salary will be determined based on the candidate’s skills and experience, in accordance with the university's salary system, and is expected to range from 3700 to 4000€ per month. This ensures a high quality of life in Helsinki.
How to Apply:

chunk_id: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT Person Job Hiring.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT Person Job Hiring.docx
text:
Job advertisement
Are you ready to join an innovative and collaborative team? We are looking for an IT Specialist to support the ongoing research efforts at the University of Helsinki, in the dynamic and exciting environment of the Färkkilä Lab. If you have a solid background in IT, and a keen interest in supporting cutting-edge scientific research, this opportunity is for you.
Administration and maintenance of cloud-based data storage, databases and computing resources used for genomics, spatial transcriptomics, and proteomics analyses. Automatization of bioinformatics pipelines for spatial analyses. IT support functions for staff having issues with aforementioned systems.
Our research
Our lab is part of the Research Program for Systems Oncology at the University of Helsinki and is deeply involved in analyzing complex datasets in cancer research. With support from prestigious funding sources like the European Research Council, the Research Council of Finland, and the Sigrid Jusélius Foundation, our team is dedicated to understanding the tumor microenvironment and how cancer cells evade the immune system.
We work with multi-omics, multi-site spatial profiling data, and functional models that have the potential to translate into clinical applications. Our research has already led to advancements in ovarian cancer diagnostics and is making an impact in the field of cancer research.
Tasks and Responsibilities
We are seeking an IT Specialist to support automated analysis pipeline development, and to manage and optimize our computational infrastructures. This position is ideal for someone who has a solid background in the topics described below. 
In this role, your key responsibilities will include:

chunk_id: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT Person Job Hiring.docx::chunk_0001
source_file: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT Person Job Hiring.docx
text:
utomated analysis pipeline development, and to manage and optimize our computational infrastructures. This position is ideal for someone who has a solid background in the topics described below. 
In this role, your key responsibilities will include:
CSC Cloud Computing Administration: Manage and create cloud environments using the CSC portal, and design cloud architecture for bioinformatic tools. Test various bioinformatic tools in these environments to determine the best configurations. Develop detailed documentation for cloud architecture setup and usage.
Data Storage Administration: Administer and optimize cloud architecture for data storage spaces and inventory hardware related to storage. Develop and update cloud data storage options (CSC, Amazon Cloud). Provide assistance with file compression and ensure the development of best practices for data storage. 
Database Administration: Develop and maintain e.g. PHP and MySQL databases for clinical and experimental data. Create and maintain manuals and documentation for database management.
Workflow System Setup and Development: Set up workflow systems (e.g., SnakeMake or Nextflow) for bioinformatic automatic pipeline analysis. Test, debug, and develop these workflow systems, and develop documentation in coordination with researchers. Set up graphical user interfaces for automatic analysis (e.g., Avivator, Jupyter Notebooks).
General IT Support: Manage and maintain databases to ensure accurate data storage, retrieval, and management. Provide technical support for researchers by troubleshooting issues related to computing environments, system requirements, and resolving software package problems, such as Python package issues.

Required Skills:

chunk_id: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT Person Job Hiring.docx::chunk_0002
source_file: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT Person Job Hiring.docx
text:
storage, retrieval, and management. Provide technical support for researchers by troubleshooting issues related to computing environments, system requirements, and resolving software package problems, such as Python package issues.

Required Skills:
Master degree in relevant topics: Bioinformatics, computer science, software engineering.
At least three years of professional experience in scientific software development in items 3 to 6 or a PhD in relevant topics.
Proficiency in python and SQL databases, skillful with PHP.
Experience with cloud architecture and cloud computing environment administration.
Strong Linux system administration skills.
Experience in using containers (e.g. Docker, Kubernetes)
Experience in bioinformatics pipeline management software (eg. Nextflow, Snakemake)
Strong logic and problem-solving abilities with keen attention to detail.
Excellent communication and documentation skills.
Desirable Skills:
Prior experience working with bioinformaticians.
Handling large data sets using Python.
What we offer
By joining our team, you will be part of a diverse and international group that is committed to improving cancer treatment. This role offers you the opportunity to contribute to impactful research at the intersection of IT and biomedical science, where your expertise will directly influence cutting-edge developments. Our research aims to make a tangible difference in the real world, improving the lives of cancer patients.
You will be part of an informal and open work culture that supports both personal and professional growth. As part of our team, you'll have access to top-tier research infrastructure and be a part of a dynamic, interdisciplinary team that prioritizes innovation and collaboration.

chunk_id: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT Person Job Hiring.docx::chunk_0003
source_file: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT Person Job Hiring.docx
text:
and open work culture that supports both personal and professional growth. As part of our team, you'll have access to top-tier research infrastructure and be a part of a dynamic, interdisciplinary team that prioritizes innovation and collaboration.
The salary range for this position is 3400 - 3900 €/month, with an expected start date on September 1st 2025, the contract will be until January 2028 with a six-month trial period. This is a full-time position, but a part-time position with minimum 60 % commitment can also be considered.
To apply, please submit the following attachments outlining your qualifications and motivation to join our team:

Schedule

1st round of interviews starting 18.8?
2nd round of interviews + decision by start of Sept
Job title options:
IT Specialist for Translational Oncology Research
IT Specialist for Biomedical Research Infrastructure

IT Support Specialist for Biomedical Research

Biomedical Research IT Specialist
IT Specialist for Biomedical Research
IT Specialist for Spatial Oncology Research

chunk_id: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT important actions.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT important actions.docx
text:
List of actions for improvements in our IT infrastructure.
L-drive and P-drives:
To check that our guidelines are in order for every folder. This is not a priority.

To find a way to synchronise your P-drive project folder with the folder in the cloud when the internet stops working. For example, some users would like to copy their project folder locally and work without the internet. Then when the internet gets activated, automatically synchronise the local folder with the cloud P-drive folder, similarly like in Dropbox but only for the folder of interest. This is not a priority.

Understand how to admin the L-drive system, when it expires, how to expand it. A postdoc Julia Casado, some years ago sent an email to get access to this system, but no information is available, the IT-helpdesk from Helsinki could have more information. Medium priority.
CSC cloud computing:
The CSC cpouta system requires updates. Cloud computers might need to be created from scratch. The size of the /home/ directory is really small, and is not enough for the users to create conda environments there. It would be important to create cloud computers with larger /home/. Also are required guidelines for the use of the cpouta computers. Also important to make a system to share conda environments across users and track the conda environments installed. Medium priority.
The CSC cpouta system utilizes so many resources. Perhaps better guidelines for activating and deactivating the instances by users are needed. Otherwise the cpoua instances are always On and wasting resources from CSC. Medium priority.

A better use of Mahti and Puhti clusters are needed in our Lab. Our Lab members have not used these systems that much, mainly Sara, and previously Olavi and Matias Aiskovich.

chunk_id: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT important actions.docx::chunk_0001
source_file: Hiring/IT person 2025/IT important actions.docx
text:
a instances are always On and wasting resources from CSC. Medium priority.

A better use of Mahti and Puhti clusters are needed in our Lab. Our Lab members have not used these systems that much, mainly Sara, and previously Olavi and Matias Aiskovich. It would be important to explore the use of the GUIs and R-studio interfaces and Jupyter notebook interfaces of Mahti and Puhti, then our Lab members would not need to install many programs locally by them and ask for extra computers for analysis. Guidelines are needed for the Lab for the use of Puhti and Mahti. Also, it’s important testing if in these GUI systems is possible to run the analysis commonly needed in the Lab, like for example image analysis. It could be good to have a centralised system for many types of analysis for the Lab, of course some analysis is better to be run locally. High priority.
Allas CSC needs to be monitored and also cleaned. Medium priority.
Databases:
Improve the architecture of PHP and MySQL clinical database of Oncosys-OVA. Create and maintain manuals and documentation for database management. Medium priority.
Create a database for spatial experiments. Medium High priority.
Webpage
Looking for cheaper hosting options High priority.
Basic routine updates (eg adding people, publications). Medium priority
Updating projects page . This is not a priority
Optimizing the website for visualization in smartphones/Tablets. This is not a priority
All these are currently handled by Ada, who can give you access Website was built with wordpress and is currently hosted in IONOS
Plan for Deb (meeting 2025.08.06): 
Within 1 week:
Get familiar with drives, check the access status, ask Julia about the L-drive;
Ask Iga to add you to our CSC project
Within 1 month: 
 With Sara -> Guideline for CSC usage

chunk_id: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/2nd interview info letter.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/2nd interview info letter.docx
text:
Welcome to the 2nd interview - with the Farkkilab! We are excited to host you!
The goal is to get to know each other better and hear more about your scientific ideas. 

The outline for the second interview is as follows:
Round of introductions (10 min)
Presentation (30 min)
Q&A (20 min) - 10-15 mins with Lab members only
Lunch with the Lab -1h 
Lab tour and 1 on 1 meetings with key personnel (30 min)
Presentation
We would ask you to prepare a max 30-minute presentation with slides, which includes
Your background and motivation
The presentation of the pre-interview task - detailed below.
Pre-interview task
This task is intended to learn more about your scientific ideas and project leadership style. Please note that there are no specific right or wrong answers to this task.
We ask you to carefully read the following article 
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39658541/
After reading the article, we would ask you to present 
1) Summary of the most relevant or interesting findings of the paper
2) Pitch a new research project based on the topic/findings/methods of the paper including
What is your research hypothesis
Which methods & samples you plan to use 
Preliminary experimental plan - how you would utilise the expertise and resources available in our lab. You can find the personnel and resources available in the lab from our website at https://farkkilab.org/. 
Main outcomes, and impact of the outcomes of the project
Q&A
The Q&A session will give our lab members a chance to ask you questions on your presentation, research, background, and motivation. After 5-10 minutes Anniina will leave the room, giving a chance for a more informal discussion about the lab in general.
Lab tour and 1 on 1 discussions
We will give you a tour of the lab facilities, resources and equipment.

chunk_id: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/2nd interview info letter.docx::chunk_0001
source_file: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/2nd interview info letter.docx
text:
ckground, and motivation. After 5-10 minutes Anniina will leave the room, giving a chance for a more informal discussion about the lab in general.
Lab tour and 1 on 1 discussions
We will give you a tour of the lab facilities, resources and equipment. We will host the 1 on 1 discussions with the lab tour, to give the key personnel working in the relevant projects a chance to talk to you in more detail.

chunk_id: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Feedback for Jie.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Feedback for Jie.docx
text:
Dear Jie,
We appreciate the time and effort you invested in our interview process, including the demanding pre-interview task. We have come to a decision after careful consideration, and unfortunately, we have decided to move forward with another candidate for the position.
The decision was difficult to make. Final scores collected from all lab members present in the interviews were close - and high, for both overall and presentation sections. We would like to provide some feedback on your interview, as well as some rationale for our decision.
Feedback on your presentation:
Your presentation was well structured, and followed the instructions that were given. 
However, selecting a more focused topic or hypothesis would have clarified your management and communication skills. For example, coming up with insights on a further development of this study or novel methodologies to apply in this setting would have been greatly appreciated by our team.
Answering questions in a more focused manner, and actively asking questions from the team would have showcased your logical thinking and organisational skills.
Rationale on our decision:
Actively asking both general and specific questions regarding, for instance, the start of the employment, the available projects, tasks, etc. would have been a way to signal your motivation towards us.
Other candidate’s background was slightly more aligned with our current needs.

chunk_id: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Hiring_process_outline.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Hiring_process_outline.docx
text:
Post-doc level hires:
The hiring process should begin with a well-defined need for a person to be hired. A hiring committee will be responsible for handling the practicalities around the hiring process.
Overall workflow for the hiring process:
Identify need -> form hiring committee (contains at least Anniina, one post-doc, and one PhD student from a relevant project, everyone is free to join if they wish)
Form a consensus profile for the potential hire within the hiring committee
Hiring committee creates and distributes the job advert
Hiring committee handles practicalities regarding interviews 
Two rounds - small group (i.e. hiring committee) interview first, full lab interview as the second round
Hiring committee presents candidates to the lab after the first round for discussion before the lab interview round
Hiring committee informs the candidate of the decisions
For each post-doc level hire, a hiring committee will be formed. 
The hiring committee consists of Anniina, at least one post-doc level lab member, and other relevant personnel who wish to join the committee. 
Everyone is free to join the committee if they wish to be a part of the process. 
The aim of the hiring committee is to handle all practical arrangements related to the hiring, and to ensure that the process is handled smoothly via having responsible persons for each part of the process, agreed upon within the community.
An important aspect for the hiring committee is to ensure communication towards the applicant(s) to create a positive experience for the applicants as well
The first task of the hiring committee is to accurately define the role of the future post-doc, and then define the profile for the new hire.

chunk_id: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Hiring_process_outline.docx::chunk_0001
source_file: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Hiring_process_outline.docx
text:
to ensure communication towards the applicant(s) to create a positive experience for the applicants as well
The first task of the hiring committee is to accurately define the role of the future post-doc, and then define the profile for the new hire.
Before posting a job advertisement, the hiring committee should be well aligned on the profile of the potential hire. As many opinions as possible should be included, and there should be a unified view on what the profile of the hire should be.
The hiring committee handles the posting of the job ad through UH HR, and additional channels, including email lists, social media etc.
The practicalities around the interview rounds are handled by the hiring committee. The first interview round should be in a small group, and a potential second round with the entire lab. Everyone is encouraged to voice their opinions on the applicant
In the case of a negative outcome, the hiring committee will inform the applicant of the decision at the soonest convenience.
The hiring committee maintains communication towards the applicants across the process to ensure a good experience for the applicants
The applications should be posted through the UH HR portal, this enables tools for the hiring committee to help with informing the candidates about the process easily
Student level hires:
The process of hiring students into the lab has been unstandardized, and the time until decision has been relatively slow. The aim of the new student hiring protocol is to create a rapid, standardised method to evaluate students. This alleviates the pressure on Anniina on assessing the flow of applications, and ensures that we are able to make swift decisions on potential students.

chunk_id: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Postdoc Fellowship in Spatial Biology and Multiomics_ Interview with hiring committee.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Postdoc Fellowship in Spatial Biology and Multiomics_ Interview with hiring committee.docx
text:
Postdoc Fellowship in Spatial Biology and Multiomics: Interview with hiring committee
The hiring committee will introduce themselves (1 minute per person). Afterward, Anniina will invite the candidate to introduce themselves and proceed with their 15-minute presentation. Anniina will also explain that following the presentation, the hiring committee will ask questions.
After the applicant’s presentation, Anniina will begin the questioning round by laying out the time expectations (e.g. “we have 6 to 8 questions for you, and we expect you to answer concisely in 3-4 min each”), and by asking the first question below. The rest of the committee members will then take turns to ask the subsequent questions on the list (AF, JJ, MH, ZK, AJ). 
Can you tell us what motivated you to apply for this position, specifically in our lab? (AF)
Which part of your research makes you feel excited? (JJ)
Where do you see yourself in 5 years and how would this fellowship help you achieve that goal? (MH)
How have the challenges you have faced - either at work or in your personal life - shaped the way you approach science and teamwork? (ZK)
What does diversity in science mean to you, and how would you promote it within our lab? (AF)
This role involves mentoring students. How do you define your approach to mentoring? (JJ)
Do you have any questions for us? (MH)
Questions for 2nd interview
If you were tasked with optimizing or developing a new spatial biology method in our lab, what steps would you take to address it? (AJ)

chunk_id: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Rejection letter AEZ draft.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Hiring/Recruitment/Experimental postdoc call 2024-2025/Rejection letter AEZ draft.docx
text:
Dear Ahmed,
Thank you for taking the time to attend the first round of interviews. We have now made our decisions based on these interviews, and unfortunately we will not proceed with your application to the second round.
We thank you for taking part in the interview process, and would like to share feedback on your interview to provide clarity on our decision and offer insights that may be helpful for future opportunities:
We enjoyed your clear presentation, and we highly appreciated your scientific curiosity to understand molecular mechanisms, and drive for an academic career. 
You have extensive experimental expertise in establishment and utilization of mouse models. However your expertise does not very closely match with our current research goals, and would perhaps be better suited to projects utilizing mouse models. 
During your presentation, it could have been more clear what your exact contributions were to each of the projects you presented. We felt that it would have been better if you focused on your first-author publications, as this would highlight your role, demonstrating the leadership and commitment to research.

chunk_id: Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx
text:
Anastasia Lundgren
24.01.2023
RESPONSIBILITIES OF A LAB MANAGER
ORDERS:
Orders: place an order and follow the orders’ status. 
Orders need to be marked to Orders Excel file, also wbs from which the order was made. For reordering the same product you copy-paste the order info from Catalog numbers file. If placing a new product order – always add the product also to Catalog numbers file for future orders.
When you receive an order – mark the arrival date in the Orders file, you can also add location of the product, for researchers, if location is not standard. Check storage temperature! On the product box/vial write lab name and arrival date+your initials. 
Antibodies – also add to Inventory file! Researchers need to know the exact location of the vial! Sometimes antibodies need to be aliquoted prior to freezing! Ask the researcher.
Bills: confirm the bill only when the actual product has arrived.
Ask PI about logics- which wbs to use for what purchases. 
Remember to use RUP basic funds wbs and ONCOSYS money.
Check if the wbs is correct in SAP.
For credit card bills collect receipts, scan them, if needed. Paper receipts are stored in a separate folder.
PLASTICS, MQ-water, BUFFERS:
Plastics to autoclave: tips, eppendorfs -mark the type on the package, we have different: Sarstedt, Eppendorf Safe-lock, Eppendorf DNA-LoBind, 1.5ml, 2ml.
Sterile MQ water for cell culture incubators: Keep minimum of 4x1L bottles on the shelf. 800ml MQ per bottle can be autoclaved.
MQ water tanks refill when close to empty. Wash canisters 4 times a year. Mark the dates.
Sterile 1xPBS: make from PBS tablets, 1-2L at a time, then divide into 500 ml and 100ml bottles and autoclave.
1xPBS tank for Färkkilä lab – make new 10L PBS from 1L 10x PBS stock+9L MQ water. Mark dates.

chunk_id: Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx::chunk_0001
source_file: Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx
text:
ash canisters 4 times a year. Mark the dates.
Sterile 1xPBS: make from PBS tablets, 1-2L at a time, then divide into 500 ml and 100ml bottles and autoclave.
1xPBS tank for Färkkilä lab – make new 10L PBS from 1L 10x PBS stock+9L MQ water. Mark dates. Wash canister 4 times a year.
CELL CULTURE ROOM:
Wash 2x cell incubators and 2x laminars minimum twice a year, or best - when Steri-Run reminder appears on the screen. 
CO2 incubator calibration is done once a year, in June, before juhannus, by lab engineers, after you washed the incubators.
Follow CO2 level at least twice a week (Mon and Fri morning). Tell Karen to order two more CO2 bottles for ONCOSYS the same day you changed the last full one.
Water bath in cell culture room– is washed once a month, we have turns. Every other turn is our turn as it is done for two labs.
Cell culture wastes: usually taken once a week on Friday afternoon, obligatory if there are patient tissue wastes. Do not leave those over the weekend.
Change of the cell culture suction bottle – upon need, usually once in one-two weeks.
CHEMICAL REGISTRY and CHEMICAL WASTES:
Chemical wastes and Chemical registry: via Fortum, twice a year or even once a year is enough. When chemical is finished and if the container has chemical registry mark on it – do not trash the package, store it until it is retired from chemical registry! New chemicals (not buffers, not biologicals) need to be added to chemical registry.
FREEZERS:
REVCOS: scrape ice 3-4 times a year. Do not defrost! Before scraping call huolto to inform that you are doing long-term door opening, so that they do not come if they receive an alarm.
Dry ice reserve is refilled once in one-two weeks, box in the Vähärautio lab Revco. It is needed for IDS samples and for any sort of samples move.

chunk_id: Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx::chunk_0002
source_file: Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx
text:
to inform that you are doing long-term door opening, so that they do not come if they receive an alarm.
Dry ice reserve is refilled once in one-two weeks, box in the Vähärautio lab Revco. It is needed for IDS samples and for any sort of samples move. is in regular use!
Keep an eye on LN storage tank in Haartman.
 You are a contact person in case of emergencies (freezers, etc.)
PATIENT SAMPLES COLLECTION AND KEEPING REGISTRIES:
Patient samples collection – if substituting a researcher. Keep patient data safe! Follow the Instructions file in ONCOSYS-Ova wiki. Mark all the relevant registries the same day! Samples table in ONCOSYS-Ova wiki, our G-drive samples table, Inventory (what was frozen: FF tissues, ascites, normal omentum) and Benchling (Benchling – as detailed notes as possible!, also copy sample photos there).
Inventories: keep up to date! When new equipment or reagent arrives always think where it should be added. 
If aliquoting antibodies, inhibitors, growth factors, etc. – place tubes into correct boxes, mark on the map and in the master file. All the notes about reconstitution and aliquoting must be added to your notebook and on the datasheet (place datasheet into the folder kept in the lab) or into the reagents journal – notebook next to the datasheets folders in the lab. If there are mistakes in calculations or chemicals used this needs to be traceable! Aliquoting for most of the reagents is done in the laminar, even antibodies. Toxic reagents are aliquoted in the fume hood, laminar is not a fume hood!
SHIPMENTS:
Shipments. UPS primarily. Always check the country regulations! Outside EU shipments (Norway is already outside EU!) – requires Proforma invoice. Needs to be filled with extra care. Prepare dry ice in advance, especially if shipping on Monday.

chunk_id: Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx::chunk_0003
source_file: Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx
text:
ENTS:
Shipments. UPS primarily. Always check the country regulations! Outside EU shipments (Norway is already outside EU!) – requires Proforma invoice. Needs to be filled with extra care. Prepare dry ice in advance, especially if shipping on Monday.
RESERVATIONS and ARRANGEMENTS:
Booking seminar rooms for lab meetings and other occasions, marking in wiki and lab calendar lab events, creating Zoom links and adding them to Lab calendar.
Booking restaurants and activities for lab events – ONCOSYS PI money are usually used. Give the place our billing info via email prior the event. We prefer being billed than paying with a lab credit card – more work in SAP.
Welcoming new people to the lab – key application, grant accesses to G-drive, wiki, Google calendar, add to Slack, mailing list. Lab tour. Computer order. Computer order for students and researchers on grants- from Helpdesk computer pool - for free.
GENERAL CLEANLINESS:
General cleanliness in ONCOSYS – cardboard, paper, batteries wastes. On Monday morning – wash bowls after Friday’s Happy hour. Call maintenance if printer is malfunctioning. Bring more printer paper from 5th floor post room. Inform Karen if tea/coffee is finishing.
In general – keep common things on the same places, return after using. 
Other people in the lab remember the locations.

text:
Spatial profiling Lab manager at the Färkilä Lab

The main tasks of the Spatial manager are to manage, record, monitor and provide assistance to the Spatial profiling (tCycIF and GeoMX) operations and experiments in the Färkkilä Lab. 
The specified tasks (% of working time allocation)
Management of resources (40%)
Reagents and antibodies
Ordering new antibodies and reagents
Keeping up the registries for all reagents, including antibody inventories and validation results. Adapting older registries to the new standards. 
Prepare commonly used reagents and buffers for research use (PBS 1x, NaOH, Citric acid buffer, alcohol chain…)
Monitoring 
use of antibodies/reagents
registries 
Samples handling for t-CycIF,GeoMX,Xenium
FFPE blocks - keeping the registry up to date
Sections cut from the blocks
Keeping track of unused sections for upcoming validation experiments or future experiments 
Assisting with slide barcoding
Organizing, storing and getting rid of the already utilized samples 
Instruments
Managing the time allocation (schedules) of Lola and GeoMX 
Making sure that the instruments are maintained properly 
Keeping the connections to the manufacturers/IT 
Troubleshooting if necessary
Ordering new parts for the machines if necessary
Revising registries of internal use and ensuring that SOPs are followed
Storage (Hard drives and P-drive)
Monitoring free space on Lola’s computer
Making sure that researchers have planned the storage of the data for the upcoming experiments 
Monitoring free space on P-drive and updating the storage-responsible person
Management of experiments (40%)
Training new students (internal and external) on the use of reagents and instruments
Tracking experiments 
Making sure new experiments are correctly planned and registered: See e

chunk_id: Lab Management team /Staff scientist absence - backup plans.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: Lab Management team /Staff scientist absence - backup plans.docx
text:
Staff scientist absence - backup plans 
Alternative contact persons/critical operations during absence of staff scientists
Anniina 
For things requiring a response in 2-3 days, you can send slack or email, I will be checking email and slack every 2-3 days.
In case you need answers within hours or minutes - you can call or send whatsapp to my phone +3585967786
Research Coordinator - Joonas
I will usually answer slack/email within a few days even on holidays
Contract matters - paivi.sinisalo@helsinki.fi, or hr-ltdk@helsinki.fi if Päivi is away. 
Keycards, access rights etc. in BM in general - anne.simola@helsinki.fi
Invoicing information/choice of wbs codes etc. internal financial matters- Saun, Aino
IT Specialist -Deb
Csc services: Access to csc project, cpouta , or information about any csc services, or CSC account need billing support. Contact: Iga [ in emergency cases email to: Kimmo Mattila (Kimmo.Mattila@csc.fi)]
Help with Data storage (Local + Network drives): Please contact Joonas. 
Data Upload (Allas & Databank): Try to organize and store them locally for the time being, it can be backed up later.
Website update: Not applicable
In any urgent matters, please Slack or Whatsapp or Call: +358451600007
Lab Coordinator -Aino
In urgent matters, please whatsapp or call +358 405031102. 
Cell incubators are alarming – two probable scenarios:
Water level too low: Fill the water tank with mQ-water.
Take a 1-liter bottle from the equipment room (next to the Oncosys overflow office) and fill it using the mQ-water dispenser (red machine next to the sink).
Open the cell incubator doors, lift the panel at the bottom, and fill the reservoir beneath the incubator floor.
CO2 levels too low: contact the 24-hour service number: 020 530 5700.
If they ask, CO2 tanks are at 2. Floor.

chunk_id: Lab Management team /Staff scientist absence - backup plans.docx::chunk_0001
source_file: Lab Management team /Staff scientist absence - backup plans.docx
text:
achine next to the sink).
Open the cell incubator doors, lift the panel at the bottom, and fill the reservoir beneath the incubator floor.
CO2 levels too low: contact the 24-hour service number: 020 530 5700.
If they ask, CO2 tanks are at 2. Floor. 
Fridge or freezer temperature rising:
Fridge: Transfer all contents to the cold room using the Färkkilä & Vähärautio cart located in the Vähärautio wet lab.
Freezer: Transfer all contents to any available freezer.
Cold room temperature rising:
Consult with any wet lab personnel or call Aino/Saun to determine what needs to be transferred out.
In general, ONCOSYS blocks and media bottles can be stored at room temperature for a day; for longer periods, they should be transferred to cold storage.
Transfer all temperature-sensitive items to the other cold room, located behind the gray door next to Sampsa’s office (directly to the left of the gray door).
Use the Färkkilä&Vähärautio cart (located in the Vähärautio wet lab space).
Other equipment issues (e.g., cell incubators, laminar hoods, cold room, fridges, freezers): contact ltdk-labengineers@helsinki.fi
Oncosys sample collection is arranged on a case-by-case basis with other sample collectors.
Spatial profiling Manager -Saun
In urgent matters, please whatsapp +358453474947. 
tCycIF consultations and Lola calendar booking matters : contact Hanna Elomaa by slack.
Orders and invoicing will be done by Aino Elomaa.
Orders will go through quartzy requests.
If you are ordering something by yourself (other than icandoc funding ) then reference is profit centre/ WBS/SAUSHAH
Invoicing for icanDoc students and other phD students in Flamma- forward it to SAUSHAH will be automatically forwarded for processing to Aino in flamma during my absence.

chunk_id: One-on one support meetings with senior scientists.docx::chunk_0000
source_file: One-on one support meetings with senior scientists.docx
text:
One-on one support meetings for Färkkilä lab
1) Concept
Students and staff may encounter various types of stressors, such as approaching deadlines, project overload, time management challenges, high self-expectations, difficulties in maintaining work-life balance, concerns about funding, or technical issues.
To identify and address such challenges early, and to offer peer-support, the senior scientists in the lab are available for confidential 1-on-1 meetings on any topic. These informal discussions are meant to provide a safe and approachable space to share concerns, gain perspectives, and feel supported. Seniors can also share personal insights on managing stress, balancing workloads, setting realistic goals, or navigating complex situations. 
Most importantly, the aim is to foster a culture of open dialogue and mutual support to reduce stress and promote work well-being.
2) Setting a meeting
Each senior team member has dedicated time slots in the lab Google Calendar. Time slots for 1-on-1 meetings with any of the senior scientists can be booked at any point by approaching anyone on the team (see vacations/conferences etc. from Google Calendar). The booking of the slot will be done discreetly if wished to be done so. If the existing time slots do not work for you, you can agree on a different time with the senior team member.
3) Senior scientists
Joonas Jukonen:
Joonas joined the lab in the summer of 2024. Joonas’ background is in molecular biology and wet lab projects, and he has worked at UH for over a decade in different labs. Joonas did most of his PhD without much support, and with constant funding-related pressure. He has worked on collaborative projects for years, often in a project lead/coordinating role.

text:
and he has worked at UH for over a decade in different labs. Joonas did most of his PhD without much support, and with constant funding-related pressure. He has worked on collaborative projects for years, often in a project lead/coordinating role. 
Joonas can assist in prioritizing important things, and identifying key steps to progress. Joonas is also happy to help in managing the mental load caused by the innate uncertainty of academic work, or to discuss any other issues causing stress, whether they are related to the science itself or not. 
Sara Palomino:
Sara joined the lab in fall 2024. She has a background in biochemistry and molecular biology, with a transition into computational biology. Her main expertise is in omics analysis. She has been involved in international, interdisciplinary projects, collaborating with professionals from diverse fields. She also has experience working in hybrid environments across two research groups, with a strong emphasis on remote collaboration. She can support the design and planning of experiments, as well as their management, including identifying key resources and contacts, delegating tasks, establishing collaborations, and defining shared areas of interest.
Hanna Elomaa:
Hanna joined the lab in spring 2025. Her background is in cell and molecular biology, and she has previously worked in an interdisciplinary research environment. Through her experience in various collaborative projects, she may offer insights into project management and setting realistic timelines. She could also provide perspectives on maintaining a sustainable work-life balance.
